# Roadmap

<!-- Structure note: this roadmap follows a WBS-inspired structure.
     Milestones represent deliverable states; bullets are work packages.
     Wave 1 gets full treatment (hierarchical IDs, Deliverable + Acceptance lines);
     later waves use abbreviated format. -->

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     WAVE 1 — in-flight milestone(s)
     Full treatment: hierarchical IDs, Deliverable line on milestone header,
     Acceptance line (or WBS-note comment) on every bullet.
     At most one or two milestones in wave-1 at a time.
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

## M1 — <name>

<!-- Example wave-1 milestone block. Replace heading, purpose sentence, Deliverable
     line, and bullets with content derived from your project context.
     Delete this comment when the milestone is filled in. -->

Goal: <one sentence describing why this milestone exists — not what it contains>.

**Deliverable:** <concrete artefact or observable state — not a restatement of the purpose sentence>.

<!--
Example bullet (delete this comment block after populating real bullets):

- [ ] M1.1 <work package — one delegatable unit of work>
  **Acceptance:** <verifiable criterion: a file path, a command output, a measurable state>.
-->

     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
